chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: 7 Years After
entities_post_resolution.definition: A customer return eligibility period stating that products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

entities_post_resolution.name: Active/Inactive Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: Status represents the lifecycle state of an order or shipment (e.g., PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status | | IS_ACTIVE | BOOLEAN | Whether customer can place orders | NO | | | IS_ACTIVE | BOOLEAN | Whether currently for sale | NO | | **Key Attributes**:
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED

entities_post_resolution.name: Bigint
entities_post_resolution.definition: BIGINT represents a 64-bit integer column type used for auto-increment primary keys such as ORDER_ID.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment) | **Primary Key**: PRODUCT_ID (INT, auto-increment) | **Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment) | **Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

entities_post_resolution.name: Category
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Category entity represented by a unique identifier (CATEGORY_ID) and a human-readable display name (CATEGORY_NAME) in the data model.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Belongs to exactly one Category | Category         → TB_CATEGORY | | CATEGORY_ID | INT | Category reference | NO | | **Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment) | | CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO |

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master(Cust Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) represents the customer master record identified uniquely by CUST_ID and used as the foreign-key target for linking sales orders.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | **Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Customer is an individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase and may receive reimbursements such as refunds for cancelled orders.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
CUSTOMER

**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID | **Key Attributes**:
- Valid email address (required for login) | **Key Attributes**:
- One or more delivery/billing addresses | **Key Attributes**:
- Geographic region for tax calculation | **Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status | - Must place at least one order to remain active | - Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type | - Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Customer | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | **Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers. | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item.Product Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: An ORDER_LINE_ITEM represents an individual product line within a sales order, identified by a line identifier and linked to the product through PRODUCT_ID.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | **Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order. | **Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment) | OrderLineItem    → ORDER_LINE_ITEM | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | ORDER LINE ITEM

**Definition**: A single line within a Sales Order specifying a Product, quantity, and unit price at time of purchase. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Product | **Key Attributes**:
- Quantity of units ordered | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order | Order Line Item Relatio

entities_post_resolution.name: Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: An Order is a sales transaction (including a customer order) that may be associated with line items and can be cancelled with a corresponding refund.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled. | **Synonyms**: order, purchase order, transaction, customer order

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Category.Parent Category Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID is the unique identifier for a product in the product master catalogue used by downstream tables such as order line items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | | PARENT_CATEGORY_ID | INT | Parent category for hierarchy | YES | | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree. | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID | Category         → TB_CATEGORY | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale. | **Foreign Keys**:
- PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | Product          → TB_PRODUCT

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Payment is a record that is associated with one Sales Order (a sales order can have one or more payments).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Sales Order Relationships**:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments | Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment          → PAYMENT | **Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Name
entities_post_resolution.definition: PRODUCT_ID is the product table’s primary key integer (auto-increment) used to uniquely identify products and link to other tables via foreign keys.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| PRODUCT_NAME | VARCHAR(300) | Product display name | NO | | **Primary Key**: PRODUCT_ID (INT, auto-increment) | **Foreign Keys**:
- PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID)

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order Hdr
entities_post_resolution.definition: Sales order header record containing one row per customer sales order, including the customer identifier (CUST_ID) and the order identifier (ORDER_ID).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
SALES_ORDER_HDR
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Header record for each customer sales order. | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Shipment is a logistics record of items sent to a customer as part of fulfilling a Sales Order, tracked by shipment identifier and shipment details such as what was sent and when.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Business Context**:
- Shipments represent the logistics side of order fulfillment. They track what items were sent, when, and to whom. | **Sales Order Relationships**:
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

entities_post_resolution.name: Eur
entities_post_resolution.definition: The per-unit price in EUR recorded at the time an order is placed, used with quantity to calculate the extended line amount.
synonyms: Unit price at time of order
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| UNIT_PRICE | DECIMAL(10,2) | Price in EUR | NO | | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price in base currency (EUR) | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

entities_post_resolution.name: Return Policy
entities_post_resolution.definition: Refund is a reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund. | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core customer records including identifiers, contact information, regional association, account status, and the date the record was created.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products including identifiers, naming, category reference, pricing, and active status for the product catalog.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: Stores hierarchical category information for products, including each category's identifier, name, and a reference to its parent category.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores header-level information for sales orders, including customer reference, order timing, total amount, status, payment confirmation, delivery timestamp, and record creation time.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores the line items for each order, including the product, quantity, unit price, and calculated line amount.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of payments associated with orders, including method, amount, status, and timestamps for confirmation and creation.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments for customer orders, including where the shipment is from, when it shipped, its tracking reference, and current status.
